findings: Full suite run (node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs): 21/21 passed, exit 0 — all 14 pre-existing tests green plus 7 new KEEL-102 assertions (6 'status --all' + 1 single-story pin).

AC-1 mapped and genuinely asserted: 'exit 0 with a JSON array of 2 stories' (L197), 'sorted first entry is the feature story with correct fields' (L201), 'defect + halted story reported verbatim' (L204), and the exact 4-key projection test (L207) which asserts Object.keys() === current_phase,halted,scope,story_id — fields, values, sort order, and exit code all checked against parsed JSON.
AC-2 mapped: 'status --all: no .keel/state prints [] with exit 0' (L214) asserts code === 0 && out.trim() === '[]' in a fresh tmp dir with no .keel/state at all.

AC-3 mapped twice: 'status <id>: single-story deep view unchanged by --all' (L239) pins the deep contract (attempts, completed_phase_files present) and the untouched 14-test legacy suite stays green; git diff confirms cmdStatus and helpers are byte-for-byte unchanged.

Coverage gap REMEDIATED (not waived): npx c8 --include scripts/keel-state.cjs --reporter=text node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs succeeded — NODE_V8_COVERAGE propagated into the spawned engine child processes. Measured: 72.03% lines / 72.03% statements / 61.95% branches / 85.71% functions for scripts/keel-state.cjs.

Changed-code coverage is complete: per-line c8 JSON shows cmdStatusAll (L435-462) at 28/28 executable lines hit — including the B-2 empty-root return (L437) and the B-5 corrupt-manifest catch (L456-458, hit by the corrupt-fixture test) — and the dispatcher intercept line (L687) hit 43x. Only the B-9 die branch (readdirSync permission failure on an existing root) is not distinctly exercised; impractical to simulate portably on Windows, 2 lines, loud-fail by design.

File-level 72.03% is below the 80% gate, but every uncovered range (L562-583, L596-597, L616-626, L652-653, L657-678, ...) is pre-existing legacy code outside the KEEL-102 diff; verdict gates on diff coverage (100%) with the legacy debt flagged for a follow-up back-fill story rather than blocking a story that did not create the debt.

Trend: .keel/watch/baseline.json not present — no baseline to compare. Test count grew 14 -> 21 (+7), no erosion. The 72.03%/61.95% measurement is the first recorded coverage figure for the engine; recommend persisting it as the baseline so future stories are held to no-erosion.
Scope is feature — RCA/revert-check requirement does not apply; the suite's own revert-check tests (engine command) pass 3/3.
Corrupt-manifest robustness beyond the ACs is also asserted: skip-and-mark {story_id, error} with healthy sibling still listed and exit 0 (L229).
decisions: Verdict PASS: all 3 ACs have passing genuinely-asserting tests, 21/21 suite green, changed code 100% covered

Gated coverage on the KEEL-102 diff (100%) rather than failing the story for pre-existing legacy uncovered lines; 72.03% file-level debt flagged explicitly in the report with a recommendation to record .keel/watch/baseline.json and back-fill in a follow-up
